channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Colossal Biosciences breeds controversy while trying to revive mammoths
item.description: A Texas biotech company is trying to bring mammoths and other extinct creatures back to life. The science is as intriguing as the ethical questions are thorny.
item.content:encoded:
A Texas biotech company is trying to bring mammoths and other extinct creatures back to life. The science is as intriguing as the ethical questions are thorny.

(Image credit: Rob Stein)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Humans' pull toward alcohol may have ancient origins (according to chimp pee)
item.description: Scientists learned that wild African chimpanzees consume alcohol by eating fermented fruit, suggesting that human attraction to alcohol may have ancient evolutionary origins.
item.content:encoded: Scientists learned that wild African chimpanzees consume alcohol by eating fermented fruit, suggesting that human attraction to alcohol may have ancient evolutionary origins.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: President Trump aimed to cut science funding. Congress has quietly restored much of it
item.description: Despite President Trump's efforts to deeply cut science funding from the federal budget in 2026, Congress quietly restored much of the funding to previous levels in recent weeks.
item.content:encoded: Despite President Trump's efforts to deeply cut science funding from the federal budget in 2026, Congress quietly restored much of the funding to previous levels in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Scientists make a pocket-sized AI brain with help from monkey neurons
item.description: A new study suggests AI systems could be a lot more efficient. Researchers were able to shrink an AI vision model to 1/1000th of its original size.
item.content:encoded:
A new study suggests AI systems could be a lot more efficient. Researchers were able to shrink an AI vision model to 1/1000th of its original size.

(Image credit: AerialPerspective Images)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: If you change the colors of a concert hall, you may change how patrons hear music
item.description: Does the color of a concert venue change how we hear music? German researchers have an answer.
item.content:encoded: Does the color of a concert venue change how we hear music? German researchers have an answer.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Peer pressure can make this clownfish change its stripes
item.description: Tomato clownfish, in response to an unpredictable world, appear capable of adjusting when they lose their stripes based on cues from other fish and their habitat, a new study in PLOS Biology finds.
item.content:encoded:
Tomato clownfish, in response to an unpredictable world, appear capable of adjusting when they lose their stripes based on cues from other fish and their habitat, a new study in PLOS Biology finds.

(Image credit: Camille A. Sautereau)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Satellite images provide view inside Iran at war
item.description: Satellite images from commercial companies show the extent of U.S. and Israeli strikes, and how Iran is responding.
item.content:encoded: Satellite images from commercial companies show the extent of U.S. and Israeli strikes, and how Iran is responding.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: NASA lost a lunar spacecraft one day after launch. A new report shows what went wrong
item.description: Why did a $72 million mission to study water on the moon fail so soon after launch? A new NASA report has the answer.
item.content:encoded: Why did a $72 million mission to study water on the moon fail so soon after launch? A new NASA report has the answer.
item.dc:creator: Joe Palca

item.title: Unlocking the secrets of an ancient plague
item.description: The first historically recorded pandemic is believed to have struck the walled city of Jirash, in what is now modern-day Jordan, in the 7th century. A new study reveals details about those who died.
item.content:encoded:
The first historically recorded pandemic is believed to have struck the walled city of Jirash, in what is now modern-day Jordan, in the 7th century. A new study reveals details about those who died.

(Image credit: Gatsi)
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: NASA redirects Artemis moon mission program, postponing a planned astronaut landing
item.description: In shaking up its Artemis lunar program, NASA's new moon plan looks more like the Apollo missions of the 1960s. Instead of landing on the surface on Artemis III, NASA hopes to do so on Artemis IV.
item.content:encoded:
In shaking up its Artemis lunar program, NASA's new moon plan looks more like the Apollo missions of the 1960s. Instead of landing on the surface on Artemis III, NASA hopes to do so on Artemis IV.

(Image credit: Gregg Newton)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne
